core_mechanism: A driven single-file stochastic hopping process of hard-core (mutually excluding) units along a one-dimensional lattice, in which localized 'slow' sites act as rate-limiting bottlenecks that throttle the steady-state throughput and reshape the density profile, with the position of and spacing between bottlenecks controlling the maximal current
brief_reason: The abstract studies a totally asymmetric simple exclusion process of size-\ell particles with slow sites of hopping rate q<1, finding the stationary current depends on the location of a single slow site and drops dramatically when two slow sites are close together, which is a directed exclusion-driven transport process governed by throughput bottlenecks.
